metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC5 prose: change 'the new four included' to 'the new five included' to match the five eval-case ids named in the What section and verified in AC2.
findings.finding: AC5 prose says 'the new four included' but the plan adds five new eval cases (R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary), creating a count mismatch between the verification prose and the actual deliverable scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 ('the new four included')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC5 to: '…which assert every dataset case (the new five included) parses under `validate_eval_spec`…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC or note stating that each of the five new eval cases in `code-review-tests.eval.yaml` must carry a `diff` field (required by `test_code_review_dataset_balanced`'s `all(c.get('diff') for c in ds)` assertion).
findings.finding: The plan's eval coverage section describes 5 new dataset cases in prose but never explicitly states that each YAML case must carry a `diff` field — yet `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` (which the plan's AC5 requires to pass) asserts `all(c.get('diff') for c in ds)`, and a case without a `diff` will silently fail that assertion at CI time.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the eval coverage section: 'Each new case must include a `diff` key with a unified-diff string (required by `test_code_review_dataset_balanced`).' Or add it as a bullet in AC5: '…including that every new case carries a `diff` payload field.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC5's count from 'the new four included' to 'the new five included' to match the five named eval cases in the 'Eval coverage to add' section.
findings.finding: AC5 says 'the new four included' but the plan specifies five new eval cases (3 recall: R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam; 2 no-fire: FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary). An implementer counting four cases to satisfy the stated AC5 would leave one case absent, causing test_code_review_dataset_balanced or validate_eval_spec(strict=True) to fail for a missing diff payload or missing id.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5: '…the new four included…'
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC5: '…which assert every dataset case (the new five included) parses under validate_eval_spec (strict)…'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the non-gating-test litmus format with the decision protocol: either relax the 'one-line litmus' constraint to allow multi-clause decision rules in the overlay (e.g. a short structured block as the existing Four-Criterion guard uses), or split the protocol into separate named litmus entries for each condition class so the full detection logic is co-located in the rubric the model sees at inference time.
findings.finding: The non-gating-test antipattern embeds a two-class conditional decision protocol (unconditional suppression vs environment-dependent condition, CI-context file-tool probe, fail-open fallback) that cannot be faithfully expressed in the 'one bolded name, one-sentence definition, one-line litmus' format the plan mandates for the overlay — the full decision rule is co-located only in the plan's 'What' section, not in the overlay rubric the model consults at inference time.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — antipattern definitions for 'non-gating test'; target file src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md
findings.suggested_fix: Model the non-gating-test entry like the existing Four-Criterion Test guard (code-review-tests.md lines 41–48), which uses a multi-line structured block rather than a single sentence. Add two sub-entries: 'UNCONDITIONAL suppression (bare skip, skipif constant-true, non-strict xfail with no env dependence): flaggable from diff alone' and 'ENVIRONMENT-DEPENDENT guard: check CI workflow files / Makefile test target; if CI context unresolvable, do NOT flag.' This keeps the litmus style while co-locating the full conditional.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the conditional sixth eval case: either commit to adding it unconditionally (name a concrete id such as `FP-hard-fail-missing-exe` and add it to AC2's for-loop) or explicitly state 'do not add it — parity holds because [reason]' so the executor has a decided path.
findings.finding: The plan conditionally requires a sixth eval case ('if that test requires parity, add one further no-fire case, e.g. a test that HARD-FAILS…') but gives only an illustrative 'e.g.' example with no committed case id, and AC2's for-loop verifies only the five named ids — so if parity IS required, the executor must invent an id with no AC anchor to validate it.
findings.location: What section — 'Eval coverage to add' parenthetical; Acceptance Criteria — AC2 for-loop
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to always add it: append `FP-hard-fail-missing-exe` to the AC2 for-loop and add a corresponding bullet in the eval coverage list. If the intent is to omit it, add a note: 'parity holds because 3 recall + 2 no-fire is already balanced per the test's definition; no sixth case needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] If the sixth no-fire eval case is a required deliverable (when `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` enforces parity), add its ID to AC2's grep-loop; if it is truly optional, remove the conditional language from the body or add an explicit note that AC5 alone gates it.
findings.finding: The plan body describes a CONDITIONAL sixth eval case (a no-fire 'hard-fail' example to be added IF `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` requires parity) but AC2's grep-loop verifies only the five named IDs (R-non-gating-skip, R-sleep-only-race, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary). If the implementer must add the sixth case to pass the balance test, no AC names its required ID, and AC2 will not validate it — leaving the conditional work uncovered by a verifiable criterion.
findings.location: What section ('if that test requires parity, add one further no-fire case') vs. AC2 (grep-loop over exactly five IDs) vs. AC5 (pytest must pass)
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) assign a definite ID (e.g. `FP-hard-fail-valid`) to the sixth case and add it to AC2's loop unconditionally (making the recall:no-fire ratio 3:3 explicit), or (b) replace the conditional language with a note: 'The balance test is satisfied by 3 recall + 2 no-fire; no sixth case is needed' and remove the conditional entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the conditional 6th no-fire case: either (a) determine definitively whether `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` requires parity and add the case (with its ID) to both the What section and the AC grep list, or (b) state explicitly that 3R:2NF is acceptable and remove the conditional language.
findings.finding: The 'What' section conditionally requires a 6th no-fire eval case (a test that hard-fails on a missing required executable) if `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` enforces recall/no-fire parity, but the AC grep list checks only 5 named IDs and does not verify this 6th case — so an implementation can pass all ACs while failing the balance test if parity is enforced.
findings.location: What section ('if that test requires parity, add one further no-fire case') vs. AC line 2 (grep for 5 IDs only)
findings.suggested_fix: Inspect `test_code_review_dataset_balanced` to confirm its parity requirement; if required, add a 6th no-fire case (e.g. id: `FP-hard-fail-on-missing-exe`) to the eval yaml spec section and append it to the AC grep list so the check becomes deterministic.

findings.finding: AC1's grep commands prove the antipattern text exists somewhere in the overlay file but do NOT verify placement in the FLAG list section vs. the false-positive-guards section specifically; a spurious match in a comment or an unrelated paragraph would satisfy `grep -ci 'non-gating' >= 1` while the structural requirement (antipattern in FLAG list, guard in FP-guards section) goes unmet.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the bare count greps with section-scoped checks, for example:
```
awk '/^## FLAG list/,/^## /' src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md | grep -qi 'non-gating'
awk '/^## False.positive.guard/,/^## /' src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-tests.md | grep -qi 'non-gating'
```
(adjust section-header anchors to match actual headings).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
